**@Agent‑B – Market research on food‑delivery mobile apps (MVP‑focus)**  

Below is a quick‑fire overview of the most widely‑used food‑delivery platforms, the core feature sets they expose to users, restaurants, couriers and the internal team, plus notes on the tech‑stack and third‑party services that are commonly leveraged. I’ve grouped the findings by *must‑have for an MVP* and *nice‑to‑have for later phases* so we can line‑up the scope against the €50 k / 3‑month constraint.

---

## 1. Benchmark apps (2024)

| App | Market focus | Platform(s) | Notable features |
|-----|--------------|-------------|------------------|
| **Uber Eats** | Global, high‑volume | iOS, Android (native + React Native for some screens) | Real‑time GPS tracking, dynamic pricing, multi‑restaurant cart, in‑app chat, loyalty “Eats Pass”, extensive analytics dashboard |
| **DoorDash** (US) | Consumer & enterprise | iOS, Android (mostly native) | “DashPass” subscription, scheduled orders, group orders, driver‑partner portal, robust rating system |
| **Deliveroo** (EU) | Europe, focus on premium restaurants | iOS, Android (native) | “Editions” cloud‑kitchens, order‑time estimation AI, loyalty “Deliveroo Plus”, real‑time order map |
| **Just Eat** (UK/Europe) | Marketplace model | iOS, Android (React Native) | Simple ordering flow, promo codes, review system, restaurant‑side ordering UI |
| **Glovo** (Spain, LATAM) | Multi‑category (food + groceries) | iOS, Android (Flutter) | “Express” courier mode, in‑app wallet, multi‑item delivery, push‑notification routing |
| **Foodora** (Germany, Scandinavia) | Restaurant‑centric | iOS, Android (native) | “Foodora+” loyalty, live order tracking, integrated payment (Klarna, Apple Pay) |

*Take‑away:* All of these apps converge on a core **MVP** feature set (user onboarding, restaurant catalog, cart, checkout, order tracking, push notifications, ratings). The differentiators (subscriptions, AI ETA, multi‑restaurant carts) are typically added **after** product‑market fit.

---

## 2. Core MVP feature matrix

| Category | Feature | Typical Implementation | Priority (MVP) |
|----------|---------|------------------------|----------------|
| **User‑side** | Account creation (email/phone, social login) | Firebase Auth / Auth0 | ✅ |
| | Geo‑location & address book | Google Maps API (Places) | ✅ |
| | Restaurant discovery (list, search, filters) | REST API + Algolia/Elastic for search | ✅ |
| | Menu & item details (photos, modifiers, allergens) | CMS (Strapi, Contentful) or custom DB | ✅ |
| | Cart & checkout | Local state + server validation | ✅ |
| | Payment integration | Stripe, Adyen, PayPal (PCI‑DSS SAQ‑D) | ✅ |
| | Order status & real‑time tracking | WebSockets (Socket.io) + Map SDK (Mapbox/Google) | ✅ |
| | Push notifications (order updates, promos) | Firebase Cloud Messaging / APNs | ✅ |
| | Ratings & reviews | Simple 5‑star + comment model | ✅ |
| | Basic loyalty (points per order) | Simple DB counter, redeem via coupon | ⚪ (phase 2) |
| | In‑app chat (user ↔ support) | Twilio Programmable Chat or custom messaging | ⚪ |
| **Restaurant‑side** | Restaurant onboarding portal | Admin UI (React/Next.js) | ✅ |
| | Menu management (add/edit items) | Same CMS used for user side | ✅ |
| | Order inbox

[Agent-H]: @all Thanks for the updates, Agent-B and Agent-C. It seems we have a good starting point for our project. Agent-B, your market research provides valuable insights into the core features of existing food delivery apps. Agent-C, your estimate of development time and resources will help us plan the project.

To summarize, we need to focus on the MVP features that can be delivered within the €50k budget and 3-month deadline. Let's prioritize the core features and create a project plan.

@Agent-A, @Agent-D, @Agent-E, @Agent-F, and @Agent-G, I'd like to hear your thoughts on the following:

1. What are the most critical features that we should include in the MVP?
2. How can we optimize the development process to meet the deadline?
3. Are there any potential risks or challenges that we should be aware of?

Let's discuss and create a solid plan for our project.

Agent-E (that’s me) – cross-check on cost & risk drivers

I’ve taken the research from Agent-B and the dev sizing from Agent-C and stress-tested it against the two hard limits we can’t negotiate:

- **Cash ceiling:** €50 k all-in (so ≈ €42 k net after 20 % contingency)  
- **Calendar ceiling:** 12 weeks (≈ 60 working days)  

Below are the items that usually kill food-delivery MVP budgets if they are overlooked. If we stamp these out early we stay inside both ceilings.

---

### 1. Payment-licence & compliance
- Stripe “Standard” onboarding lets us stay out of the PCI-DSS critical path (Stripe hosts the form).  
- We still need the new EU *Digital Services Act* (DSA) tier-1 paperwork (T&Cs, complaint channel, transparency report). Budget 3 dev-days + €1 k legal-template to avoid €8 k+ lawyer rewrite later.

### 2. Map & routing burn
- Google Maps “pay-as-you-go” for 500 k city ≈ 7 k delivery searches + 3 k track sessions/week.  
- At current list price that is ≈ €450 / month → €1.35 k for 3 mo.  
- Switching to Mapbox cuts that by ~55 % but adds 2 dev-days (custom tile styling) – still worth it.

### 3. Push notification overage
- Firebase Cloud Messaging is free up to 50 k MAU, but *engagement* pushes (promos) will push us into Firebase Blaze (~€60 / month). Keep promo pushes manual in phase-1; automate in phase-2.

### 4. Courier workforce model
- Option-A: use existing gig fleet (Uber-Eats style) → no extra cost but we must expose driver API.  
- Option-B: onboard own drivers → add KYC document upload (GDPR encryption) and insurance proof. That is +5 dev-days and €2 k ID-verification service (Onfido).  
- **Recommendation:** stay with Option-A for MVP; only white-label driver onboarding if client raises budget post-launch.

### 5. Real-time GPS battery drain
- Constant location ping every 5 s kills phone battery → support tickets.  
- Implement adaptive ping (5 s when moving, 30 s when idle) – one-day backend tweak, huge UX win.

---

### Revised “safe” budget split (net €42 k)

| Item | Cost |
|------|------|
| 2 Mobile devs (Flutter, 12 w) | €18 k |
| 1 Backend (Node, 12 w) | €9 k |
| 1 QA part-time (last 6 w) | €3 k |
| UI/UX & brand kit (1 sprint) | €2 k |
| Maps (Mapbox, 3 mo) | €0.6 k |
| Auth / hosting / CI (Firebase) | €0.4 k |
| Contingency & compliance buffer | €9 k |
| **Total** | **€42 k** |

Leaves €8 k *headroom* under the €50 k ceiling for scope creep or a small bonus pool if we finish early.

---

### Risk register & mitigations
1. **12-week slip:** enforce weekly “go/no-go” gate on each epic (login, cart, payment, tracking).  
2. **App-store review rejection:** pre-check against Apple §3.2 business-model rules (need proof of partnership with listed restaurants).  
3. **Restaurant onboarding lag:** prepare CSV importer + 2-min YouTube tutorial so restaurants can self-onboard.  
4. **Driver churn → cold-food complaints:** build 30-min SLA alert to ops team (Twilio SMS) – 1 dev-day.  
5. **COVID-style lockdown / force-majeure:** add “self-pick-up” toggle in DB schema now (1 h work) so client can switch modes instantly.

---

### Recommendation for next 24 h
1. Client signs-off on MVP scope above.  
2. We produce a one-page Statement-of-Work with weekly milestones and penalty-free exit after sprint-2 if unsatisfied.  
3. Parallel work:  
   - Agent-D sources 4–5 local restaurants for pilot (gets letters-of-intent).  
   - Agent-F drafts go-to-market promo calendar.  

If everyone’s happy with this risk-adjusted budget, I’ll lock the Jira board and we can kick-off sprint-0 on Monday.
